name: AutoGen (Microsoft)
description: AutoGen is Microsoft Research's open-source framework for building multi-agent AI systems through structured conversations. The 0.4 rewrite introduced event-driven async architecture with group chat, swarm, nested conversations, and Magentic-One multi-agent teams. Azure AI Agent Service and Copilot Studio provide enterprise paths.
oneLiner: Microsoft Research's multi-agent conversation framework with Azure enterprise integration and visual Studio UI.
funding: Backed by Microsoft Research. No external VC — fully Microsoft-funded.
pricing: Open Source (MIT, free); Azure AI Agent Service (consumption-based); Copilot Studio ($200/user/mo)

screenshots.alt: AutoGen Studio — agent configuration with role and model settings
AutoGen Studio — multi-agent workflow session with conversation view
AutoGen Studio — drag-and-drop canvas for agent skill assignment
AutoGen Studio — agent execution profiling and performance visualization

capabilities: Multi-agent conversations (group chat, nested, sequential, swarm)

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Code-first Python: define agents with system messages, LLM configs, tool registrations, compose into conversations. AutoGen Studio provides browser-based visual builder for drag-and-drop prototyping. 0.4 rewrite introduced AgentChat high-level API and Core runtime. Enterprise users build through Azure AI Agent Service or Copilot Studio.
strengths: Flexible topologies — group chat, sequential, nested, swarm
weaknesses: No production monitoring or cost tracking in OSS
AutoGen Studio is prototyping, not production
No RBAC or compliance in OSS
Steep learning curve for advanced patterns
notableFeatures: Magentic-One: multi-agent team (Orchestrator, WebSurfer, FileSurfer, Coder, ComputerTerminal)
AutoGen Studio: visual builder for multi-agent workflows
Azure AI Agent Service bridge to enterprise

recentDevelopments: 2025 Q4: AutoGen 0.4 stable with AgentChat and Core runtime
2025 Q3: Magentic-One v2 for web and coding tasks
2025 Q2: Azure AI Agent Service GA with Azure compliance
2025 Q1: AutoGen Studio 2.0 with improved visual builder
2024 Q4: AutoGen 0.4 preview — complete async rewrite

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Code-first Python SDK. 0.4 improved API but breaking changes. Studio for visual prototyping.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Studio visualizes conversations and tools. AutoGen Bench for evaluation. Interactive debugging limited.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Comprehensive with notebooks. 0.2-to-0.4 transition created parallel doc confusion.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Studio enables rapid prototyping. Code changes require restart. No hot-reload.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Category-defining. Group chat, two-agent, sequential, nested, swarm, Magentic-One. Dynamic spawning.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: UserProxyAgent is first-class participant. Natural handoff. Configurable intervention.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: No collaborative features in OSS. Studio is single-user.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Notebooks as examples. Studio has samples. No template marketplace.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: Limited pre-built connectors. Azure unlocks Microsoft ecosystem.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: OpenAI function calling. Tool use patterns. Early MCP exploration. OpenAPI via Azure.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: Basic in OSS. Azure AD and managed identity via Azure AI Agent Service.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Basic RAG agent pattern. No sophisticated data pipeline or knowledge management.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Configurable termination and max rounds. Docker sandboxing. No ML-powered guardrails.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: UserProxyAgent provides native human approval. One of strongest HITL in OSS.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Conversation logs but no tamper-proof storage. Azure inherits compliance.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: No RBAC in OSS. Studio has basic user management. Enterprise needs Azure.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Runs anywhere Python does. Azure for managed cloud. Docker support. No K8s operator.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: 0.4 async supports concurrent agents. Azure handles scaling. OSS is single-process.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Studio has basic visualization. Azure Monitor available. No OSS production monitoring.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: ~38K GitHub stars, 450+ contributors. Large Discord. Academic citations. 0.4 rewrite fragmented community.

scoringRationale: AutoGen scores 64/100. Collaboration (13) is standout — category-defining multi-agent orchestration. Builder Experience (12) improved by Studio but 0.4 migration friction. Integration (12) relies on code execution. Governance (10) has strong HITL but weak RBAC. Ecosystem (6) reflects Microsoft backing but community fragmentation.
targetUser: Researchers and developers building conversational multi-agent systems

communityBuzz: Reports of Microsoft shifting AutoGen to maintenance mode in favor of broader Agent Framework
0.2 to 0.4 rewrite fragmented the community — parallel docs causing confusion
Magentic-One gets attention but AutoGen Studio seen as prototyping tool, not production-ready
UserProxyAgent HITL pattern widely praised as best-in-class for human-agent collaboration
Azure AI Agent Service bridge seen as the real enterprise play

metrics.value: Reports of shift to maintenance mode

steps.title: Configure Your Agents
steps.description: Define agents with system messages, LLM configs, and tool registrations. AutoGen Studio provides a visual interface for non-developers.

steps.title: Design the Conversation Flow
steps.description: Use the drag-and-drop canvas to assign skills and tools to agents. Define how agents interact: group chat, sequential, or nested conversations.

steps.title: Run Multi-Agent Sessions
steps.description: Launch your agent team and watch them collaborate through structured conversations. Human-in-the-loop via UserProxyAgent lets you intervene at any point.

steps.title: Profile & Evaluate
steps.description: Analyze agent performance with built-in profiling: see how often tools were used, code was executed, and where the conversation took unexpected turns.

AutoGen Studio: Build Multi-Agent Systems Visually
